questions.question: What is the key difference between problems in class P and class NP?
expectedPoints: P problems can be solved in polynomial time
NP problems can be verified in polynomial time
All P problems are in NP, but not necessarily vice versa

questions.question: Explain what it means for a problem to be NP-complete and why this classification is important.
expectedPoints: Definition: NP-complete = in NP and NP-hard
NP-hard: every problem in NP can be reduced to it
Importance: identifies the hardest problems in NP
Practical significance: helps choose appropriate algorithms
Connection to P vs NP problem: solving one NP-complete problem in polynomial time would prove P = NP
